terms.definition: Scalar index (0–1) measuring alignment of a civilization’s subsystems and its capacity for coordinated action
Critical coherence level below which self-correction becomes statistically implausible; analogous to a phase-transition point
terms.first_use_section: What We Know – The Threshold is Real

terms.definition: Mean annual proportion of coherence lost to structural drift, conceptually borrowed from thermodynamic entropy production
Exogenous inflow of ordering energy attributed to transcendent authority that offsets entropy and enables coherence restoration
A reality or moral order existing outside the closed socio-cultural system, posited as the source of grace
Collective admission of incapacity, re-orientation toward transcendent authority, and behavioral surrender effectuating β-activation
Codified behavioral code in Old Order Amish society that maintains high grace coefficient through communal discipline
Amish theological posture of willful surrender or yieldedness, functionally equivalent to β-activation
